title: crepis neglecta subsp. graeca
intro: Crepis neglecta subsp. graeca, also known as the Greek hawk's beard, is a plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Crepis neglecta subsp. graeca include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health, as well as its antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage. Crepis neglecta subsp. graeca can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
